papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.contribution: Shows reasoning abilities emerge via pure RL (GRPO) without supervised traces; emergent self-reflection behaviors.
papers.engagement: 92K GitHub stars; 443 HF upvotes; #1 Paper of the Day

papers.title: s1: Simple test-time scaling
papers.contribution: Achieves test-time scaling with only 1,000 SFT examples + 'budget forcing'; beats o1-preview by 27% on AIME24.
papers.engagement: 6.6K GitHub stars; 124 HF upvotes; #1 Paper of the Day

papers.title: Think Deep, Not Just Long
papers.contribution: Introduces Deep-Thinking Ratio (DTR) showing token quality matters more than quantity; Think@n reduces cost ~50%.

papers.title: Does RL Really Incentivize Reasoning Capacity in LLMs Beyond the Base Model?
papers.contribution: RLVR does NOT elicit new reasoning -- base models achieve higher pass@k; RL merely biases distribution.

papers.title: The Illusion of Thinking
papers.contribution: LRMs exhibit 'reasoning cliff' -- performance collapses beyond a complexity threshold.
papers.engagement: Extremely viral on X/Twitter; generated published rebuttals

papers.title: Reasoning Models Don't Always Say What They Think
papers.contribution: CoT faithfulness is low (<20% hint reveal rate); RL-trained models improve but plateau.

papers.title: Inverse Scaling in Test-Time Compute
papers.contribution: Constructs tasks where longer reasoning HURTS accuracy; identifies 5 failure modes.

papers.title: Spurious Rewards: Rethinking Training Signals in RLVR
papers.contribution: RLVR works even with random/incorrect rewards -- MATH-500 improves 21.4% with random reward vs 29.1% with ground truth.
papers.engagement: Highly provocative; questions RL signal necessity

papers.title: GSM-Symbolic
papers.contribution: LLMs show significant variance when numerical values change; performance declines with added complexity.

papers.title: MATH-Perturb
papers.contribution: 'Hard perturbations' cause all models including o1-mini to drop 16.49%, revealing technique memorization.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning In The Wild Is Not Always Faithful
papers.contribution: Unfaithful CoT occurs on natural prompts -- models give coherent justifications for contradictory answers. GPT-4o-mini: 13% unfaithful; frontier thinking models: <1%.

papers.title: SFT Memorizes, RL Generalizes
papers.contribution: RL with outcome-based reward generalizes across textual/visual variants while SFT memorizes; SFT still essential as stabilizer.
